old_claim.note: Green Revolution provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because commercial hybrid seed production predates the Green Revolution and is better modeled as an upstream breeding capability.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical review section describing 1910s hybridization techniques and the 1926 Hi-Bred Corn Company.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review places commercial hybrid seed production in the 1920s, before the mid-20th-century Green Revolution programs.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That chronology refutes Green Revolution agriculture as a prerequisite for hybrid seed production; the relationship should point historically from hybrid breeding capabilities toward later yield programs, not backward.

ontology_before: Hybrid seed production depended directly on the later Green Revolution node, forcing hybrid seed chronology to postdate a program it helped enable.
ontology_after: Hybrid seed production now depends on seed selection and Mendelian genetics only; Green Revolution agriculture can still use breeding capabilities downstream.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant added in 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps the Green Revolution edge absent and locks the corrected 1926 hybrid seed date.
would_reject_if: The hybrid_seed_production node were narrowed to mean only Green Revolution-era hybrid seed programs.
A stronger historical source showed commercial hybrid seed production did not exist until after the Green Revolution began.
A later graph refactor introduced a more precise downstream node for Green Revolution hybrid seed deployment and moved this dependency there.
short_reason: Commercial hybrid seed production predates the Green Revolution, so the old dependency direction was reversed.
